content.problemStatement:
Do you know what  b'ak'tun means ? Its an enormous cycle in the Mayan calendar, roughly around 400 solar years. The 13th b'ak'tun is going to end this December and our chef Remy is worried about it. What can a chef do if the world is going to end soon ? Enough of making recipes, time to visit the restaurants around the world. He prepares a list of restaurants along with their ratings. This is given as a 2D array A having R rows and C columns. A[i][j] has the rating of the jth restaurant in the ith row. Rows are numbered 1 to R from top to bottom and columns are numbered 1 to C from left to right.



Remy can start his journey from any restaurant in the top row ( row# 1 ) and end at any restaurant in the bottom row ( row# R ). He is afraid that the world may end from top to bottom, and so he will never go up from a restaurant. He can only move left, right or down ( i.e., to cell (i,j-1) or (i,j+1) or (i+1,j) ) in each step. Along the path he takes, he sums up the ratings of all the restaurants visited. Of course, each visited restaurant's rating should be added exactly once, even if he visits it again along the path. Find the maximum possible sum of ratings.

( Read the Input section for details on reading / generating A )
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing three integers R, C, and I, where R is the number of rows, C is the number of columns, and I is the input method. If I == 1, the next R lines contain C integers each, representing the grid A. If I == 2, the next line contains four integers X, P, Q, and M, used to generate the grid A.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the maximum possible sum of ratings along a path that starts in the top row and ends in the bottom row.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
The path with maximum sum of ratings in Case 1 is shown in bold: 
1 1 -5 **3** **1**
-1 **1** **1** **-1** **3**
2 -3 **2** -1 1
The path with maximum sum of ratings in Case 2 is: 
10 13 13 29

The path with maximum sum of ratings is: 
1 2 3
4 5 6
7 8 9

The path with maximum sum of ratings is: 
10 13
13 29

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 2D array dp of the same size as the input grid A, where dp[i][j] represents the maximum sum of ratings that can be obtained by starting from the top row and ending at cell (i, j). We initialize the first row of dp with the values from the first row of A. Then, for each cell (i, j) in the grid, we calculate dp[i][j] by adding the maximum sum of ratings from the cells above it (i-1, j-1), (i-1, j), and (i-1, j+1) to the value of the current cell A[i][j]. Finally, we find the maximum value in the last row of dp, which represents the maximum possible sum of ratings along a path that starts in the top row and ends in the bottom row.
solution.timeComplexity: O(R * C) per test case, O(T * R * C) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(R * C) — the dominant structure is the 2D array dp.
